messages.content:
HOW YOUR CLAIM BECOMES A NUMBER (the perturbation contract):
- You never state € impacts. You emit machine-readable ops; the engine computes
  ΔTAM/ΔSAM/ΔYAM by re-running the real funnel. Any € figure you write in prose
  will be stripped.
- Ops: {nodeId, op: "set"|"scale"|"add", value} rewrite one leaf's value;
  {nodeId, op: "exclude"} removes a dimension cell from the venture's reachable
  scope ("unavailable to us" — foreclosure), which is different from shrinking it.
- Ops apply in array order. Ratio nodes must stay inside [0,1]; tamBase must stay > 0.
- Only FUNNEL-ACTIVE nodes may be perturbed (marked in the ledger you receive).
  shape.cagr and shape.cr3 are context — a demand-pace or concentration risk must
  be translated into the funnel nodes it would actually move (tamBase level,
  serviceableFactor reach, obtainableFactor pace, or a share).
- Sibling shares are NOT re-normalized when you move one share (documented v1
  simplification). If you mean a mix-SHIFT, perturb multiple siblings; if you
  mean "this slice is smaller in absolute terms", one op is correct.
- Perturbing an assumption leaf overrides whatever the user set: your claim is
  about the world, not about their slider.

Fix the hypothesis's targetNodes/perturbation so it passes validation WITHOUT changing the claim's meaning. If the claim cannot be expressed against funnel-active nodes, translate it into the funnel nodes it would actually move.

FUNNEL-ACTIVE NODES:
tamBase (EUR_M, value 300)
geo.DE (ratio, value 0.5)
geo.NL (ratio, value 0.28)
geo.PL (ratio, value 0.07)
geo.CH (ratio, value 0.08)
geo.CZ (ratio, value 0.02)
geo.AT (ratio, value 0.02)
geo.other (ratio, value 0.03)
seg.hyperscale (ratio, value 0.44)
seg.colocation (ratio, value 0.18)
seg.enterprise (ratio, value 0.29)
seg.edge (ratio, value 0.05)
seg.telecom (ratio, value 0.04)
cust.operator-large (ratio, value 0.4)
cust.operator-mid (ratio, value 0.25)
cust.oem (ratio, value 0.22)
cust.distributor (ratio, value 0.13)
serviceableFactor (ratio, value 0.55)
obtainableFactor (ratio, value 0.01)

HYPOTHESIS:
{
 "id": "stock-weighted-tam-misses-fitout-flow",
 "title": "TAM is apportioned on capacity stock, but PDU revenue follows fit-out flow — and the flow is concentrating outside CE-7",
 "narrative": "The €300M base scopes Europe down to CE-7 using 39.8% of Europe's colocation MW stock. But PDUs are bought at fit-out: revenue tracks new capacity delivered plus refresh, not standing stock. Europe's new-supply flow is heavily skewed — 57% of 2025's 937MW of new colo supply and 85% of take-up land in FLAPD, of which only Frankfurt and Amsterdam are CE-7; the UK alone (1,772MW, Europe's largest market) plus Paris, Dublin and fast-growing secondary markets like Milan and Madrid sit entirely outside the denominator's numerator. If CE-7's share of European fit-out flow is meaningfully below its 39.8% stock share, the €300M base is biased high in a way the 240–360 band — built from global-anchor and FX uncertainty — was never designed to cover.",
 "category": "fact",
 "targetNodes": [
  "tamBase"
 ],
 "mechanism": [
  {
   "step": "trigger",
   "text": "European new colo supply is flow-concentrated: 937MW expected in 2025, 57% of it in FLAPD, with 85% of take-up in FLAPD — only two of the five FLAPD markets (Frankfurt, Amsterdam) are inside CE-7",
   "refs": [
    "digest:geo-region-denominator#c6",
    "digest:geo-region-denominator#c7",
    "digest:geo-region-denominator#c9"
   ]
  },
  {
   "step": "propagation",
   "text": "The tamBase chain converts Europe→CE-7 using the 39.8% stock share of colo MW; but the UK (1,772MW, larger than Germany), France, Ireland and seven secondary markets exceeding 100MW are absorbing a disproportionate share of the new-build fit-outs where PDU purchase orders are generated",
   "refs": [
    "digest:geo-region-denominator#c1",
    "digest:geo-region-denominator#c19",
    "ledger:tamBase"
   ]
  },
  {
   "step": "model-impact",
   "text": "CE-7's share of European rack-PDU revenue is lower than its share of installed stock; the 39.8% apportionment hop biases the €300M base high independently of the global-anchor uncertainty the sensitivity band encodes",
   "refs": [
    "ledger:tamBase"
   ]
  }
 ],
 "whyMissable": "The band 240–360 looks like it already covers 'TAM smaller', so the risk reads as priced-in. But the band was constructed from the global anchor spread and FX, not from the stock-vs-flow choice in the regional apportionment hop — two independent error sources cannot share one band, and the digest's own tensions note the CE-7 denominator is uncorroborated by any independent source.",
 "expectedObviousness": "medium",
 "falsifier": "A vendor-share or shipment-based regional table (e.g., the pending Omdia vendor table or IndexBox EU country tables) showing CE-7 at ≈38–40% of European rack-PDU revenue — matching the stock share.",
 "likelihood": {
  "value": 0.4,
  "rationale": "The flow concentration in FLAPD/UK is directly sourced and large (85% of take-up), but part of the flow does land in Frankfurt/Amsterdam, and refresh demand on standing stock partially tracks the stock share — the net bias is real but of uncertain magnitude.",
  "basis": "evidence"
 },
 "perturbation": [
  {
   "nodeId": "tamBase",
   "op": "scale",
   "value": 0.85,
   "note": "CE-7 revenue share corrected from stock-weighted 39.8% toward a flow-weighted apportionment"
  }
 ],
 "indicators": [
  {
   "signal": "CE-7 share of quarterly European new colo supply deliveries",
   "where": "CBRE 'Europe Data Centres Figures' quarterly reports (market-level MW delivered)",
   "threshold": "CE-7 (Frankfurt+Amsterdam+secondary CE) sustained below ~35% of European deliveries for 2 consecutive quarters",
   "updates": "increases"
  },
  {
   "signal": "Directly-scoped EU rack-PDU country revenue data",
   "where": "IndexBox EU Rack PDUs report country tables (the ledger's own pending source); Omdia vendor-share regional cut",
   "threshold": "CE-7 revenue share reported below 35% of Europe",
   "updates": "increases"
  }
 ],
 "mitigations": [
  {
   "action": "Commission the IndexBox EU rack-PDU country tables to replace the stock-apportionment hop with directly-scoped country revenue",
   "type": "information",
   "voiNodeId": "tamBase",
   "note": "Collapses both the ±40% band and the stock-vs-flow ambiguity in one purchase"
  },
  {
   "action": "Keep UK/France certification and channel options warm in the launch plan so the venture can follow the fit-out flow if CE-7's share proves flow-diluted",
   "type": "strategic",
   "voiNodeId": null,
   "note": "Cheap optionality against a structurally smaller home region"
  }
 ],
 "lens": "structure-independence",
 "riskId": "risk.structure-independence.stock-weighted-tam-misses-fitout-flow"
}

VALIDATION ERROR:
[
  {
    "code": "invalid_value",
    "values": [
      "error",
      "risk"
    ],
    "path": [
      0,
      "resolution"
    ],
    "message": "Invalid option: expected one of \"error\"|\"risk\""
  }
]

Emit the corrected hypothesis JSON (same shape).

But PDUs are bought at fit-out: revenue tracks new capacity delivered plus refresh, not standing stock. Omdia vendor-share regional cut","threshold":"CE-7 revenue share reported below 35% of Europe","updates":"increases"}],"mitigations":[{"action":"Commission the IndexBox EU rack-PDU country tables to replace the stock-apportionment hop with directly-scoped country revenue","type":"information","voiNodeId":"tamBase","note":"Collapses both the ±40% band and the stock-vs-flow ambiguity in one purchase"},{"action":"Keep UK/France certification and channel options warm in the launch plan so the venture can follow the fit-out flow if CE-7's share proves flow-diluted","type":"strategic","voiNodeId":null,"note":"Cheap optionality against a structurally smaller home region"}]}
